# Questions in Livestream 83

Live Stream #83: Now is the Winter of Our Discontent

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=631" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:31</a> | Why does 1 Samuel mention both the temple and the king before Samuel is even born? | He said Hannah's words are prophetic, while `temple` in 1 Samuel 1 reflects the later writer describing the Shiloh cult complex with familiar language. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=1038" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:18</a> | Why are rabbits unclean in Leviticus 11:6, and should the passage say they eat grass rather than chew cud? | He said it should not be translated as eating grass. Rabbits do chew in a cud-like way through their digestive cycle, though not like cows. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=1406" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:26</a> | Why is Samson never credited with liberating Israel from the Philistines? | Because he did not. He struck the Philistines, but he was ultimately captured and failed to bring Israel lasting peace. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=1581" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:21</a> | What happened to Mentuhotep II's mortuary temple? | He said very little remains because much of its stone was quarried away, especially for Hatshepsut's nearby complex. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=1832" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:32</a> | In Genesis 1:1, does the Hebrew support creation ex nihilo or building from chaos? | He did not take the chaos view. He said the language suggests a blank-canvas formlessness, while not by itself requiring creation ex nihilo. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=2156" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:56</a> | Did Snefru use labor practices resembling American slavery, and how severe was slavery under him? | He said Snefru likely used war slaves from Canaan for pyramid projects, but this was not the same as American chattel slavery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=2481" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:21</a> | Were girls in ancient Israel married around age 12 or 13, and how should that be morally evaluated? | He said the ancient context was shaped by survival, mortality, family protection, and arranged marriages, so modern categories should not be imposed simplistically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=3095" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:35</a> | How should one answer a Hebrew Israelite claim that egyptology is false if the first Egyptians were not black, and that pyramids were not BCE because the Bible does not say so? | He described Egyptians as broadly Hamito-Semitic with regional variation and said Egyptian pyramid building clearly belongs before the Common Era. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=3510" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:30</a> | Are there extra-biblical historical records of Moab, Ammon, and Edom being judged like Babylon or Tyre? | He said not really. Moab, Ammon, and Edom have limited surviving texts, so the biblical record remains the primary source for much of this material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=3703" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:43</a> | What scholars, books, or historians would he recommend for studying the historicity of Judges? | He recommended Kenneth Kitchen's work on Old Testament reliability and Richard Hess's `Ancient Israelite Religion`, then following their bibliographies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=3856" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:16</a> | If full preterists appeal to Scripture alone against the creeds, should Christians listen to the church, and is sola scriptura at fault? | He said full preterism is outside orthodox faith, but sola scriptura is not nuda scriptura: church tradition is a consulting voice, while Scripture has final authority. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=4518" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:18</a> | What advice does he have for overcoming vices such as lust, gluttony, and pride? | He distinguished ancient gluttony, righteous and unrighteous pride, and lust inside versus outside marriage, then emphasized Scripture, prayer, and spiritual discipline. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=5231" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:11</a> | How can Jesus be said to have been born in 4 BC? | He said it is a calendar artifact: Dionysius Exiguus miscalculated the epoch, and later evidence placed Jesus's birth a few years earlier. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=5429" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:29</a> | Did ancient Egyptians think they came from somewhere other than Egypt? | He said no. Egyptian myth presented Egyptians as native to the land, created and placed in Egypt rather than migrating from elsewhere. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=5586" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:06</a> | When is he going to play guitar on stream? | He said he uses the guitar mostly as a composition tool, not for live performance, so viewers should not expect him to play on stream. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=5821" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:01</a> | Does Genesis 19:36 mean there were no Moabites or Ammonites before Lot's daughters gave birth to their ancestors? | He said not necessarily. The sons of Lot are presented as pivotal defining figures for those nations, not necessarily their only biological origin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=6057" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:57</a> | Were the Exodus plagues polemics against Egyptian gods? | He said no. He traced that view to a modern scholarly theory and argued that it lacks both strong Egyptological support and direct biblical warrant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=6605" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:05</a> | Is there a secret ancient library under the Sphinx? | He said no. Ground checks and restoration work show no such passages or library under the Sphinx, though he wished more texts existed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=6860" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:20</a> | What does he think of Dorothy Eady's reincarnation claims, and would possession by ancient dead spirits conflict with Scripture? | He rejected reincarnation as unbiblical, said consulting the dead is forbidden, and suggested possession is possible but not by deceased human spirits. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=7377" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:57</a> | Does a claim about Haman as an Egyptian priest in the Quran prove the Quran true? | He said no. He treated the claim as unsupported and said he knew of no such Egyptian record from the reign of Ramses II. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=7614" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:54</a> | How can the clan explanation work with biblical frameworks such as Adam being 130 when Seth was born or Noah's sons entering the ark at 100? | He said Hebrew literature can aggregate figures or retroject famous progenitors, so several clan-based explanations could account for those details. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=7979" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:59</a> | Has he seen the Indiana Jones trailer? | He had seen it and thought it looked bad, especially because Harrison Ford no longer fit the role as an action hero. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=8117" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:17</a> | How did Moses know about Adam, Noah, and Abraham? | He said Moses likely knew through oral tradition passed down among the sons of Jacob, with oral transmission preserving narratives while updating vocabulary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=8248" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:28</a> | If the Star of Bethlehem appeared after the Messiah's birth, how did Herod determine the child's age range? | He said the Magi were seeking a recently born king, so Herod used their report and travel timing to target infants two years old and younger. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=8423" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:23</a> | What are his thoughts on replacement theology? | He said replacement theology has serious problems because Romans 11 shows God is not done with Israel, and not all promises to Israel transfer to the church. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=8685" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:45</a> | How does he handle Joshua 11:11 in light of his statement about Hazor not being burned in Judges 4? | He said Joshua 11 and Judges 4 refer to different periods. His point was that Judges 4 does not claim Hazor was burned in Deborah's time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=9062" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:02</a> | Does Genesis 1:28's use of `subdue` carry violent connotations? | He said he would need a fuller word study, but the term seems to involve force or coercion, with violence possible by extension. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=9191" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:11</a> | Do aquariums and bird cages have a long history, and how far back do they go? | He said fish ponds are ancient, true glass aquariums are much later, and bird cages go back to ancient Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=9401" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:41</a> | What is a one-liner response to the claim that there is no archaeological evidence for the Exodus? | He said to ask what evidence exists instead, because absence of evidence is not itself evidence against the Exodus and positive evidence still needs explanation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=9754" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:34</a> | What might a new Reformation in the church look like? | He said the exact form is hard to predict, but it would need a renewed esteem for Scripture and responsible, regular Bible reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=10112" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:32</a> | What does he think of reading Genesis 1:1 as a restricted relative clause? | He was not comfortable with it and said it feels like an unnatural way for a Semitic text of this kind to begin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=10318" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:58</a> | How does the missing tomb of Cleopatra compare with the tombs of other Ptolemaic rulers? | He said most Ptolemaic tombs are also unaccounted for, so finding those first would be a better strategy for narrowing Cleopatra's burial location. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=10633" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:13</a> | How did the Egyptian language evolve, and how is Demotic both a script and a late form of Egyptian? | He distinguished language stages from scripts, saying Demotic is mainly a script for Ptolemaic Egyptian and is often loosely conflated with the language. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=11108" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:08</a> | Does God approve Jehu's destruction of Ahab's house in 2 Kings 10:30 but condemn it in Hosea 1:4? | He said it is not a contradiction: Jehu did some commanded judgment, but also went beyond the commission in the bloodshed at Jezreel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=11800" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:40</a> | Will he make a video responding to comparisons between Jesus and Osiris? | He said maybe, but popcorn-review targets are chosen selectively based on length, topic, educational value, and copyright risk. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=11949" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:19:09</a> | Is he aware of the Galilean earthquake of 363 AD, and does he think God intervened to stop construction of the Third Temple? | He said he was aware of the earthquake, but thought it more clearly affected the Temple of Jupiter and did not prove a stopped Third Temple project. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=12160" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22:40</a> | What are his thoughts on claims that the Old Testament is developing henotheism, historical allegory, or too human in quality? | He rejected the Old Testament as mere historical allegory or developing henotheism, favored monarchical monotheism, and said human qualities are expected in Scripture addressed to humans. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=12751" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:31</a> | Could the Egyptians really do magic like the Westcar Papyrus and Exodus suggest? | He said Egyptians practiced many things called magic, from sleight of hand to ritual and pharmacology, but it is hard to know what was actually effective. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=13116" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:36</a> | What was Jesus doing before his public ministry? | He said Jesus worked as a carpenter, likely in the normal inherited trade of his father's household. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=13426" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:43:46</a> | Who is more knowledgeable, Indiana Jones or Titus Kennedy? | He declined to answer directly because of the recent controversy, saying viewers could come to their own conclusion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=13533" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:33</a> | Why does secularism feel tired and limiting? | He said the problem is a shallow, fundamentalist-style way of viewing the world, which can also appear in some forms of Christianity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=13756" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:49:16</a> | What are his thoughts on Richard Friedman's Levite Exodus theory? | He was skeptical, mainly because he rejects the documentary hypothesis framework and sees timing and distribution problems with a small Levite-only Exodus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=14001" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:21</a> | What does 1 Peter 3:7 mean by calling women the weaker vessel? | He said it is a euphemism for a precious, fine vessel such as glassware: something honored and cared for, not treated roughly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=14427" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:00:27</a> | What does he think of Messianic Judaism holding to Old Testament traditions and practices, and how does that relate to Galatians? | He said Messianic Judaism varies widely. Some groups are healthy, but legalistic versions resemble the kind of law-dependence Paul opposed in Galatians. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/46uDNoLqNA8?t=14875" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:07:55</a> | How should one answer claims that Ramses II spoke with Haman, the high priest of Amun, to build a tower and then drowned? | He said there is no evidence for that Quranic version of the Exodus and no biblical warrant for Pharaoh drowning. |
